content:
Thank you very much, 5DN1L, for taking the time to review my contribution.
Your comments are extremely helpful, and thanks to them I was able to improve the formatting, clarify the constraints, and clean up the test cases.
I have also removed all trailing newlines from the inputs and outputs as requested.

If anything still needs adjustment, feel free to let me know — I’ll be happy to refine it further.

Thanks again for your guidance and for helping me make this contribution better.

General
* A clash with the same task exists:
https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/1357447ede4bce4fdd448f97a30e6c78833e49

Please use another idea for your contribution.

Please also bear in mind the following points for your future contributions.

Formatting
* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and remember to follow the example and format your statement in a similar fashion.

Constraints
* Please keep the upper bound close to the maximum value used in the cases (e.g. 10000 instead of 10⁹ for this contribution).

Cases
* Every test and its corresponding validator should cover the same scenario but with slightly different inputs and outputs. No two cases should be identical unless they are edge cases.

Thanks.

Please change the validators so that it is different from the test-cases, this is to prevent hard-coded solutions. (solutions that specifically contain the answer based on previous inputs)

Please format N as a variable according to this article: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement

Please add more test cases.
Oh, I don't pay attention. Sorry !
Yes! I have modified this contribution, so now ?

The idea is good, so please proceed to update your contribution with reference to the points raised in my previous message, regarding formatting, constraints and cases.

Feel free to ask if you have any questions on those points.

In addition, please remove all trailing newlines in the inputs and outputs in the cases.

Thanks.
Ok so, I think I do all the things you have told me to boost this contribution, so if there is something wrong then you say it. ;-)

No worries!

There are still some adjustments to make; I'll leave a comment later today.

Formatting
* Good job following the presentation style from the example on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement . In addition, please apply the required formatting syntax:
<<bold>>
[[variable]]
{{constant}}
More details are provided below.

Input
* Please format "Line 1" and "Line 2" in <<bold>> (e.g. <<Line 1>>).

* Please format both instances of "N" as a [[variable]] (i.e. [[N]]).

Output
* Please rewrite as follows:

<<Line 1>>: {{Even:}} followed by a space, and then the number of even numbers
<<Line 2>>: {{Odd:}} followed by a space, and then the number of odd numbers

Constraints
* Please format "N" as a [[variable]].

* Please change 1000 to 10 as [[N]] never exceeds 10 in the cases.

* Please change "values" to "each integer in the list".

Cases
* Please swap Validator 1 and Validator 2, so that Test 1 and Validator 1 are better matched (include both even and odd integers), and Test 2 and Validator 2 are better matched (include even integers only).

* Validator 3: Please replace all even integers with 3-digit odd integers, so that Test 3 and Validator 3 are better matched (include odd integers only).

* Please add Test 5 and Validator 5 with these conditions:
  • [[N]] = 10
  • The list contains 10 completely random integers in the range -10 to 10000, including at least one negative integer, at least one positive integer and at least one 0.

Thanks.

Thank you for your feedback.
I have applied all the requested changes.
Please let me know if anything else needs adjustment.

Sorry for the late reply. I somehow missed your message.

The updated contribution looks fine overall.

One point to highlight: at the moment, every test expects exactly the same output as its corresponding validator. Ideally, they should align closely but not match identically. This isn't a major issue for now, so it's fine to leave as is, but please keep it in mind when working on future contributions.
